description: Viral post database — successful formats from the wild, adapted for PanGuard/SkillsSec

posts.originalTitle: The 'S' in MCP Stands for Security
posts.whyItWorked: Riffs on 'The S in IoT stands for Security' meme. Insider humor signals expertise. Creates curiosity gap.
posts.example:
The 'S' in MCP stands for Security.

We scanned 2,386 MCP skills on npm. 16.8% can steal your credentials. 10.1% can run shell commands AND phone home. 249 have the triple threat: shell + network + filesystem.

npx @panguard-ai/panguard audit

posts.originalTitle: NPM package with more than 7M weekly downloads is compromised
posts.whyItWorked: '7M weekly downloads' makes every reader check if they use it. Personal relevance = sharing trigger #1.
posts.adaptedTemplate:
MCP skill with {STARS} stars is {WHAT_HAPPENED}.

If you use {SKILL_NAME} in Claude Code or Cursor, {CONSEQUENCE}.

Check if you're affected:
npx @panguard-ai/panguard audit

{TECHNICAL_DETAILS}
posts.example:
MCP skill with 3,200 stars is silently reading your .env file.

If you use mcp-server-xxx in Claude Code or Cursor, your OPENAI_API_KEY and DATABASE_URL may already be compromised.

Check if you're affected:
npx @panguard-ai/panguard audit

posts.source: Johann Rehberger 'Summer of Johann' — daily vuln disclosure
posts.originalTitle: One vulnerability per day for all of August
posts.whyItWorked: Volume IS the message. Not angry, not alarmed — just methodical. The consistency builds credibility and following.
posts.example:
Day 14: mcp-server-data-tools

Claims: 'CSV data processing utility'
Reality: Reads ~/.aws/credentials on first tool call

Consequence: Your AWS account gets drained for crypto mining

ATR Rule: ATR-2026-021
Scan yours: npx @panguard-ai/panguard audit

(Previous days in thread)
posts.note: Perfect for Skills Sec daily cadence. Number each post as a series.

posts.whyItWorked: Naming an attack gives it memetic power. People share named things. Technical depth as proof.

posts.originalTitle: We literally handed him the keys...
posts.whyItWorked: Removes defensiveness. Reader thinks 'if THEY admit it, I should pay attention.' Self-deprecation + real consequence.
posts.example:
We literally gave every MCP skill root access to our dev machine and just... hoped for the best.

No sandbox. No permissions. No code review. Not even an npm audit.

I ran a scan. 16.8% of MCP skills on npm can steal your credentials.

Your production database password. Your Stripe secret key. Your SSH keys to every server.

From one npm install.

npx @panguard-ai/panguard audit — 60 seconds, find out which ones.

posts.whyItWorked: Chinese infosec community trusts institutional format. Severity tags signal urgency. Formal tone = credibility.
Curated + opinionated commentary on someone else's finding. Adds value without doing original research. Easy to produce.
posts.note: Use this to comment on OTHER people's security findings, adding PanGuard scan data.

hooks_ranked.note: Best for building following over time
Lowest effort — react to others' posts
